Table 4: L6 exemplar matrix. H = High, M = Medium, L = Low. ∗LP structurally inaccessible (clones possess no future). †ME collapses at death but constitutes the story’s subject throughout. ‡MR suspended: narrative refuses moral adjudication.
Table 11: Genre comparison across three analytical dimensions (detailed)
Table 13: Scoring rubric for dimension evaluation

Figure 2: Score convergence trajectories across five iterative rounds for each layer (L4–L6) and genre category. Scores stabilize rapidly by Round 3–4, with canonical and pulp fiction consistently scoring higher than LLM-generated stories across all layers and rounds.
Figure 3: Genre comparison across three analytical layers. Error bars indicate standard deviations. All pairwise differences between canonical and LLM-generated stories are significant at p < 0.001.
Table 5: Evaluation corpus composition and distribution

Figure 1: SAGE framework architecture. (a) Six-layer hierarchy: Layers 1–3 assess textual properties through rule-based metrics (dashed); Layers 4–6 evaluate interpretive dimensions through LLM-based analysis (solid). (b) Evaluation pipeline: each story is assessed in two modes (content-limit and title-limit) across three layers via a dual-track architecture comprising a five-round iterative evaluator and an independent validator, with inter-rater agreement analysis.
